think from your experience, timing-wise, what makes sense from a timing perspective for a content engine to actually get started?

Series A, C, et cetera.
At least what I've seen is where you have some clarity on where you need scale.
It can be one of the early signals of where something like this can make a bit more sense.
Is there real pain?
Is there real connection and a strategy we plug into?
Or are we at the phase where you need more help in really developing that strategy?
And that doesn't mean it's simple black and white equation in terms of like, is this going to be the right time or not?
But certainly more the former just makes it a lot easier to look at the opportunities and say, where are we going to jam?
Where can we help unblock you in a variety of ways?
You know, what I've at least seen is like, and obviously this is going to bring in some of my own biases, but waiting too late can have some really detrimental effects.
I have been at a variety of different stops and my background essentially is like how to help teams like broaden beyond becoming reliant on just paid search.
And so like a lot of the stops I've been have been like, hey, we're spending way too much on paid search.
It's getting super, super expensive.
Like we tried some tests on some other channels, LinkedIn, the Facebooks, whatever it may be, varying effectiveness there.
So usually it's like how to make some of the paid channels be more repeatable outside of paid search and then how to unlock organic to really not be as reliant on paid.
Where I have seen that come too late in the journey, it just has a lot of complexity.
But, you know, what that looks like for each business really depends on like your goals and like your current plans to get to those goals.
And so, you know, certainly I see I see value in investing in the early phases of a journey, but it's it's more about like, hey, you know, if you have clarity of like what is the go to market motion.
We're going to support if you've tested a few different things, have some motion that can help capture demand in a variety of ways.
That makes it all much easier for us to show short-term success, right?

And usually to get that buy-in on the budget from the team too, like recognizing that we're not going to be the cheapest agency out there, that just tends to make the proposition kind of a no-brainer at that point when we start to look at opportunities.
